shortDescription.text: Admin-only endpoint accessible to any authenticated user

fullDescription.text: The GET /benefits route requires only isLoggedIn middleware but omits the isAdmin check. The isAdmin middleware is present in a comment but not applied, allowing any authenticated user to access the admin-only benefits management page. This is a broken access control vulnerability (OWASP A01:2021).

shortDescription.text: All non-admin users' benefit data exposed without authorization

fullDescription.text: The displayBenefits handler calls benefitDAO.getAllNonAdminUsers(), which returns benefit details for every non-admin user in the system. Combined with the missing access control, any authenticated user can enumerate all other users' benefit information. This constitutes an information disclosure / excessive data exposure vulnerability.

shortDescription.text: Response hardcodes isAdmin: true regardless of actual user role

fullDescription.text: The benefits route handler passes { isAdmin: true } in the template render data regardless of the requesting user's actual role. This misleads the client-side into rendering admin UI elements for non-admin users, which combined with the missing server-side access control creates a complete authorization bypass for the benefits page.

message.text: The GET /benefits route is registered with only isLoggedIn middleware: app.get('/benefits', isLoggedIn, benefitsHandler.displayBenefits). The isAdmin middleware is commented out and not enforced, allowing any authenticated user to access admin-only benefits management. Fix: add the isAdmin middleware to the route chain: app.get('/benefits', isLoggedIn, isAdmin, benefitsHandler.displayBenefits).

The displayBenefits handler invokes benefitDAO.getAllNonAdminUsers() which returns benefit data for every non-admin user in the system. Because the route lacks admin authorization, any logged-in user can retrieve all other users' benefit details (start dates, salary, tax information). Fix: enforce isAdmin middleware on the route and consider returning only the requesting user's data for non-admin users.

The benefits route handler passes hardcoded { isAdmin: true } in the response template data regardless of the actual user's role. This causes the client-side template to render admin controls for all users, and masks the missing server-side authorization check. Fix: derive the isAdmin flag from the authenticated user's session or database record: { isAdmin: req.session.isAdmin || false }.
